item.title: Whelp I shot my shot.
item.description:
Context: I just thought she was cute had never met her and had not chatted with her before handing her the note. She had some cool tattoos.
Fun roller coaster of emotion today! It was a nice confidence boost for me as well! It didn't work out this time due to the age gap. But that doesn't mean it never will! If you like someone you should say something! Get out there and shoot your shot!
item.dc:creator: SomeSkepticalDude

item.title: Doctors know that when they hear a patient say “this is the worst headache of my life!” It’s time for an emergency brain scan.
item.description:
This is a little PSA involving an uncommon event known as a “thunderclap headache.” A thunderclap headache is called such because it comes on suddenly, and intensifies within 60 seconds. The actual headache lasts longer than five minutes, and it is EXCEEDINGLY PAINFUL. It feels like you’ve been struck with lightning. Patients often describe it as “the worst headache of my life.” In fact, patients have used this phrase so much that a legitimate symptom of a thunderclap is saying “the worst headache of my life.” Thunderclap headaches are *often* a result of bleeding and swelling in or around the brain. Sometimes it really is just a random headache, but you can’t know that until you talk to a doctor. If it’s a result of bleeding, it is NOT going to get better until you have immediate medical attention. In fact, it will get WORSE.
This is a less blurry version of the first image (link found in sources cited). Some people might write a thunderclap headache off as a migraine, especially those with chronic migraines. However, a thunderclap headache is different. Fortunately, I found a table that explains the differences.
Source: https://migraineprofessional.com/thunderclap-headaches-vs-migraines/
You don’t want to fuck around with a brain bleed or injury. The longer it goes on, the higher the risk of the damages becoming more severe and permanent.
The table in the first image is from the Advanced Headache Center: https://www.advancedheadachecenter.com/what-are-thunderclap-headaches I also took a look at this page, but it’s filled with A LOT of dense medical language: https://practicalneurology.com/articles/2023-may-june/headaches-not-to-miss-regardless-of-the-patient-population-thunderclap-headache-in-the-emergency-department Harvard Health Publishing - Note that I could only read the first few paragraphs before I hit a paywall: https://www.health.harvard.edu/diseases-and-conditions/the-worst-headache-of-your-life
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

item.title: A Winter Court Opinion
item.description: Seriously, if you like it so much go outside, and live with your choice. FP Edit: To clarify, I don't hate the "I love summer" people as long as they're out and about in the heat, like I am in the winter. Some of you are just looking for someone to be mad at; you're welcome. Send me things in memory of @HowLongHaveIBeenLurking if you wanna send things.
item.dc:creator: VashTehStampede

item.title: The two basic types of "Christian"
item.description: I know this post may get downvoted into oblivion, but I like this Christian litmus test. Let's us not forget others like Dolly Parton ... Jimmy Carter, Fred Rogers, etc. These people don't feel the need to tell people they are Christian. They have no need to remind people of their faith. Their actions show it every day. Dolly's Imagination Library: https://imaginationlibrary.com/check-availability/?gclid=EAIaIQobChMIwc-P2s76hgMVEWdHAR18Ogy2EAAYASAAEgIZj_D_BwE Image courtesy of @goodgodabove

Part l

item.title: A pose a day: Doorway Reaching Up Hanging Entering Room
item.description: Hi! I'm AdorkaStock. I photograph myself and others in various poses for art reference. My goal is to include in my galleries of stock poses a variety of body types, skin types, and people doing various things so there is a piece of stock for everyone. I want to share my stock, which is free to use for reference, with as many people as possible in case they need it! FAQ is in my bio. WE ARE AT 75% OF OUR $1,000 GOAL! But only 4 days left in the month. Thank you so much for your help raising money for The Trevor Project, The Theater Offensive, and Transgender Emergency Fund MA. Please consider picking up a pack if you want to help out! Packs are $5+ (you can tip more and add more to the goal!) and you get 187 poses to work with in exchange. :) https://ko-fi.com/s/b8b57a8648 Hope this pose helps you! Happy drawing! #Art #AdorkaStock #Reference #Artist User tags: @HaikuSorrow @Brestaris @The9mm @Veraatje01 @thegraphiteknight @CanOnlySayYes @SmolTenk @5GFPLaqZ4nAy2QwrqOEI @Nautas @LadyAzzura @Noooooozzzzzzzz @BeanTootsAreBest @pumpkinknight @sniggly5212 @TheMemeForge @lgwingnut @Talon83 @KootchieKoo @roeliebobs @TheDizzyToast @pangalgargblast @IndianCowboy @cojamist @BobbyBobbston @BenderRodriguz1010 @Netcat @ScurvyMI @BrutalAnalSeepage @AlmostPerfectComment @CouldntCakeLess @Hedgehoglet @iDrawStuff @PprHrt @Genuphobatastic @brandopolaf @TorisArt @Wormwoodworms @HikariShade @ICGer @whitegirlcanteven @demoncatmara @zeroyoungxray
item.dc:creator: adorkastock

item.title: My grandma is gone
item.description:
My strong and courageous grandma has lost her battle with Parkinson's and congestive heart failure. It was a long decline, but she had her family by her side through it all. She was cracking jokes up til the very end. She was trying to talk to my mom, who had her earbuds in, so grandma started singing that she has beans in her ears.
I would like to get this photo of her edited without the oxygen hose. I also plan to attempt to do this in charcoal (never done before, but I am artistic, so I want to give it a go) she left behind quite a legacy. I have such a beautiful and big family.
The morning of her funeral outside my aunt and uncle's house. She's off to the spirit in the sky to be reunited with my grandpa. She was widowed young at 42 and raised 6 kids on her own. My uncle says they never wanted for anything because of her strength and determination. That's the Finnish sisu and boy, she had it.
My grandma enjoyed my nature photography, so in her honor, I'm going to post some here.
I was very blessed to have her living across the street from me for many years. I could bake with her and sleep over. She'd pay me a dollar to run a toy car over her arms and legs, she liked the tickle feeling. I do too, my sister and I would do the same gently running the tips our fingers over eachother's backs. I now gently trace my finger over my son's face to help him fall asleep. He says it feels good.
She moved to South Carolina when I was young so I was not able to see her as much as I would have liked after that.
She never got to meet her great grandson, and it pains me that it took her funeral for me to make that trip. The time and money seemed too great at the time. I wish I'd tried harder to find a way.
I got to see a lot of aunts, uncles, and cousins that I haven't seen in decades.
I want to take my aunt, who I lived with for 6 months post high school graduation, up on her offer to visit her in Florida. Time is short, and family is everything.
I got to stay with my brother and sister in law while I was there.
It feeds the soul to be with my siblings, who are my best friends. We're big into tabletop and card games.
We played cards and talked deeply about raising our kids, politics, and philosophy.
Anyway, I think I rambled enough. Hug your loved ones and keep them close.
Doggo tax, my brother's dog Rhaeny (GOT fans)
She's quite the love bug
Cat tax. My sister's cat Mishkin also got some lovings. He's a skittish rescue that only likes women. (Possibly trauma/abuse in his past from men) Edit: thank you all who have commented with such kind words
item.dc:creator: Spookycabbages

item.title: Just Passing By. Tourists at the beach pointing at a Manatee
item.description: By far the most common comment I get when I post videos of sharks, manatees or rays swimming really close to people in the water is "how could they not see it?". Some people just don't believe they wouldn't see it, as if their eyes work differently than everyone else. However, whether the water is clear or murky, it's often hard to see below the surface of the water because of sun glare and wind disturbance, especially at lower angles. That's why people make polarized glasses, clear boats, and viewing cones. If you want to know what's beneath the surface of the water, wear polarized glasses. All of the pics and videos I post are taken by me. For more OC nature vids check out my gallery at https://imgur.com/user/SeeThroughCanoe/posts or look for See Through Canoe Company on YouTube & other social media sites.
item.dc:creator: SeeThroughCanoe
